attempts.response:
Below is a SAPIC+ model of the described Needham–Schroeder public-key protocol (NSPK). It models two roles (Alice/initiator and Bob/responder), uses asymmetric encryption, and includes events to reason about secrecy of the nonces and a basic authentication property relating Bob’s commit to Alice’s start. It also includes an executability lemma to ensure a successful run exists.

```spthy
theory NSPK_English_Translation
begin

builtins: asymmetric-encryption

/* Roles
   - Alice(ltKa, pKb): Initiator with private key ltKa talking to Bob's public key pKb.
   - Bob(ltKb, pKa):   Responder with private key ltKb talking to Alice's public key pKa.

   Messages
   1) A -> B: aenc(<pk(ltKa), Na>, pKb)
   2) B -> A: aenc(<Na, Nb>, pKa)
   3) A -> B: aenc(Nb, pKb)

   Security intent (modeled by events/lemmas)
   - Secrecy of nonces Na, Nb unless a long-term key is compromised.
   - A basic authentication: If Bob commits on (A,B,Na,Nb), then there was an
     earlier start by Alice with the same (A,B,Na) (or a long-term key was revealed).
*/

let Alice(ltKa, pKb) =
    new Na;
    let pKa = pk(ltKa) in
    event AliceStart(pKa, pKb, Na);
    out(aenc(<pKa, Na>, pKb));
    in(c2);
    let <Na2, Nb> = adec(c2, ltKa) in
    if Na2 = Na then
      event AliceAccept(pKa, pKb, Na, Nb);
      out(aenc(Nb, pKb));
      event AliceCommit(pKa, pKb, Na, Nb);
      event Secret('Na', pKa, pKb, Na);
      event Secret('Nb', pKa, pKb, Nb)
    else
      0

let Bob(ltKb, pKa) =
    let pKb = pk(ltKb) in
    in(c1);
    let <pKaR, Na> = adec(c1, ltKb) in
    new Nb;
    event BobRunning(pKb, pKaR, Na, Nb);
    out(aenc(<Na, Nb>, pKaR));
    in(c3);
    let Nb2 = adec(c3, ltKb) in
    if Nb2 = Nb then
      event BobCommit(pKb, pKaR, Na, Nb)
    else
      0

let Compromise(ltK) =
    event RevLtk(pk(ltK));
    out(ltK)

process:
!(
  new ltKa; let pKa = pk(ltKa) in out(pKa); event HonestA(pKa);
  !(
    new ltKb; let pKb = pk(ltKb) in out(pKb); event HonestB(pKb);
    (
        Compromise(ltKa) | Compromise(ltKb)
      | ( !Alice(ltKa, pKb) | !Bob(ltKb, pKa) )
    )
  )
)

/* Lemmas */

/* Executability: There exists a trace where both parties reach their commits. */
lemma executable:
  exists-trace
  "Ex A B Na Nb #i #j.
      AliceCommit(A, B, Na, Nb) @ #i
    & BobCommit(B, A, Na, Nb) @ #j
    & #i < #j"

/* Secrecy of nonces (conditioned on no long-term key reveal). */
lemma secrecy_Na:
  "All A B Na Nb #i.
      AliceCommit(A, B, Na, Nb) @ #i
    ==> (not (Ex #k. K(Na) @ #k))
        | (Ex #r. RevLtk(A) @ #r)
        | (Ex #r. RevLtk(B) @ #r)"

lemma secrecy_Nb:
  "All A B Na Nb #i.
      BobCommit(B, A, Na, Nb) @ #i
    ==> (not (Ex #k. K(Nb) @ #k))
        | (Ex #r. RevLtk(A) @ #r)
        | (Ex #r. RevLtk(B) @ #r)"

/* Basic authentication from Bob's point of view:
   If Bob commits on (B,A,Na,Nb), then some Alice previously started with (A,B,Na),
   unless a long-term key was compromised. */
lemma auth_from_Bob:
  "All B A Na Nb #i.
      BobCommit(B, A, Na, Nb) @ #i
    ==> (Ex #j. AliceStart(A, B, Na) @ #j & #j < #i)
        | (Ex #r. RevLtk(A) @ #r)
        | (Ex #r. RevLtk(B) @ #r)"

end
```

Notes:
- Public keys are derived as pk(ltK). We model agent identities by their public keys.
- The Compromise role emits RevLtk(pK) and leaks the private key, enabling lemmas to condition on uncompromised cases.
- The secrecy lemmas are stated on nonces Na and Nb under the standard Dolev–Yao adversary, conditioned on no long-term key reveal.
